title: Example PRD Review Bundle
summary: Give maintainers a deterministic, portable PRD artifact that can be reviewed in a browser and committed with its companion assets.

problem.statement: Single-file prose templates make document structure and asset handling depend on the authoring agent.
evidence: The current workflow asks an agent to reproduce template structure directly.
Review artifacts need to work outside a specific coding-agent harness.

goals.goal: Produce the same basic bundle from the same manifest.
goals.success_signal: The command creates index.html and copied assets under action-items/PRD-example-review-bundle/.

personas.need: Generate a review artifact with one documented command.
personas.outcome: The artifact is portable across conventional macOS and coding-agent shells.

requirements.title: Portable bundle
requirements.description: The generated HTML resolves CSS, JavaScript, and SVG through relative paths.

decisions.decision: Use Python 3 and the standard library.
decisions.rationale: The generator should not require package installation or Codex-specific runtimes.

testing_strategy.expected_outcome: index.html and every referenced local asset exist in the generated directory.

rollout.outcome: Maintainers can generate and inspect a basic bundle before the richer block catalog is added.

risks.risk: A partial write could look like a complete PRD.
risks.mitigation: Validate first and publish a temporary bundle only after all files are written.

open_questions: Which additional visual surfaces should be selected for the next iteration?

repository_grounding.observation: The existing skill produces a styled HTML PRD.
repository_grounding.implication: The bundle generator should preserve browser-readable output while separating deterministic rendering from agent judgment.
